entry.title: Re: Disable generic layout buttons in ALV grid

entry.title: Re: How to Manage Multiple Delivery Points from a Single...
entry.content: Thank you for your help and for the link to the very interesting blog. However, I haven't found the answer to my multiple delivery problem. I understand that the delivery location needs to be specified in the customer order. But let's take an example where a customer orders 10 machines and wants me to deliver 5 machines to address 1 and 5 machines to address 2. How should I proceed in SAP? Do I need to create 2 lines in my customer order, one line per destination? Or is there a different way to do this? Generally, I don't always know how many delivery locations the customer will request when I'm entering the customer order.

entry.title: Re: 🚨 SAP Build Apps – Access Denied (403 Forbidden) – S...
entry.content: Yes , Its resolved now . Thanks for the update Yogananda.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to download a screen (dialog program) to file?
entry.content: Menu only available when you click on DIsplay for Element List in SE51

entry.title: Connecting to a Database server on the internet
entry.content:
I am using SAP BTP Trial account to test deploying an app through SAP BTP Cloud Foundry. I have built the docker container and pushed into the SAP BTP cluster using CF push. However, the app does not start saying that it cannot connect to the Database. The app needs to connect to a MongoDB database running on a server hosted elsewhere on the internet. That server is accessible from other places and even running the image locally, works.

I tried setting up Destinations, but cannot get it to work. Can anyone help? Is what I am trying possible? An app deployed on BTP Cloud Foundry, talking to a database hosted on the open internet?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Organizational Unit error for business partner
entry.content:
Hi @SFC 
I brought this to the attention of our SAP expert who recommended:
Please check the step to maintain purchasing information when creating the carrier CAR-020.
Please remind the carrier to maintain this information (e.g. currency):


Does this help?
Kind regards
Margit

thanks for your reply and the related notes. I know how to download and install the ADT and BW-MT.

We don't want to use HANA Studio anymore.

entry.title: Re: CDS View for 2LIS_11_V_SSL and 2LIS_12_VCSCL?

entry.title: Constraint net cannot be deleted (PMEVC)
entry.content:
Constraint net cannot be deleted

Freitag, 31. Januar 2025

 

Usecase for deleting the constraint net:

 

First create a dependency net (aka constraint net aka CN)

 

App: PMEVC (GUI-App)

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Save

 

 

 Then create constraint into the new CN

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Then release constraint and CN and save

 

Later, the CN can be removed from profile:

 

 

 

 

Works fine, on a first glance:

 

 

 

 App: CT04 (GUI-App)

 

 

However, the cstic AVC_ROBO_TYPE is still existent in the where-used list:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Now trying to cleanup:

 

When double clicking the CN AVC_RBT_CN_TEST_001_A can be displayed:

 

App: CU03 (GUI-App)

 

 

Display mode works fine

 

However, when switching to change mode…..

 

 

 

 

Then the error occurs:

 

 

How can we delete no longer needed constraint nets to ensure housekeeping?

entry.title: Re: SAC & BW live with parallel queries: Unable to retrieve data from the data source
entry.content:
Hi!
It seems that we are currently facing the some issue/problem.
Could you already solve your described problem?
Best regards,
Bernhard Thüringer

entry.title: Business Partner(BP) Complete Configuration Guide in SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
In this blog, we’ll dive deep into the essentials of Business Partner configuration in SAP S4 HANA.

A business partner can represent an individual, an organization, or a group. It is defined by a fundamental structure that includes “business partner categories”. In SAP S/4 HANA, the Business Partner serves as the central object and a unified entry point for managing master data related to business partners, customers, and suppliers (previously referred to as vendors). This streamlined approach simplifies data maintenance and enhances consistency across the system.

Step 1: Define Business Partner Grouping

In SAP S/4 HANA, Business Partner Grouping is a key concept used to categorize and organize business partners based on specific criteria, such as their role (customer, vendor, or partner) or business requirements.

For example, you can create groupings like "Domestic Customers," "International Vendors," or "One-Time Suppliers" to streamline master data management and ensure consistent processing. These groupings are assigned during business partner creation and influence fields, screens, and functionalities available for each partner type.

Path: SPRO-> IMG-> Cross-Application Components-> SAP Business Partner-> Business Partner-> Basic Settings-> Number Ranges and Groupings-> Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges.

Step 2: Define Vendor Account Group

In SAP S/4 HANA, a Vendor Account Group is a key configuration element that categorizes vendors based on their roles and business requirements. It determines the control parameters for vendor master data, such as number ranges, field settings, and screen layouts.

Account groups ensure that vendors are managed consistently and efficiently, whether they are one-time suppliers, regular vendors, or international partners. By defining specific account groups, organizations can tailor vendor master records to meet their unique operational needs, ensuring smoother procurement processes and better data governance. Proper configuration of vendor account groups is essential for optimizing vendor management in SAP S/4 HANA.

Path: SPRO->IMG-> Financial Accounting->Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable-> Supplier Accounts-> Master Data-> Preparations for Creating Supplier Master Data-> Define Account Groups with Screen Layout (Vendors).

For example, Business requires reconciliation account as mandatory for Import Vendor.

 Select Z001 and navigate to details, then Double click on Company Code Data

Double click on Account Management

Make reconciliation account is required entry for Import Vendor

Step 3: Assign number ranges to vendor account groups

Path: SPRO->IMG-> Financial Accounting->Accounts Receivable and Accounts  Payable-> Supplier Accounts-> Master Data-> Preparations for Creating  Supplier Master Data-> Assign Number Ranges to Vendor Account Groups.

Step 4: Business Partner & Vendor Account Mapping

Path: SPRO-> IMG-> Cross-Application Components-> Master Data Synchronization-> Customer/Vendor Integration-> Business Partner Settings-> Settings for  Vendor Integration-> Field Assignment for Vendor Integration-> Assign Keys-> Define Number Assignment for Direction BP to Vendor.

Click on New Entries, enter the grouping and account groups and tick on same Nos. as shown.

Step 5: Create Sample Import Vendor

Chose Z001 -Import vendor and enter all required data and select the BP role as per requirement

Click on Purchasing

Enter the required data in purchasing screen

Select Company code and enter the required data.

Vendor 11012 Created.

Step 6: Business Partner Role Configuration

In SAP S/4 HANA, Business Partner roles define the functions or relationships a business partner can have within the system, such as customer, vendor, or employee. Configuring these roles is essential to ensure accurate and efficient master data management. Each role determines the attributes, data views, and processes associated with the business partner. Assign the relevant attributes and partner functions to each role, ensuring alignment with your business processes.

BP Role

Title

Description

FLVN00

FI Vendor

Business Partner Vendor

FLVN01

Purchase Vendor

Business Partner Supplier

BP Role Grouping

SPRO->IMG-> Cross-Application Components-> SAP Business Partner-> Business Partner-> Basic Settings-> Business Partner Roles-> Define BP Role

Click New Entries and enter Role Grouping Name, navigate to BP Role Groupings -> BP Roles.

Click on New Entries

Assign the BP Roles as per the requirement and then save the data

Step 7: Let's Create a Vendor sing BP Role Grouping

Navigate to Transaction BP, Select the created BP Role.

All the required views like Company code, Purchasing and General Data will appear in a single screen.

Enter the Company Code details

Enter the Purchasing Data Details and save the BP

BP 11014 Created.

entry.title: Delivery Relevant Sales Items with Billing Plan
entry.content:
I am looking for a solution to this:

Customer orders x 10 of an item at £150.00 each.

No set delivery date. Customer will advise as and when they are to be delivered this year (when needed). They will be dispatched via standard delivery processes.

But billing will not be delivery related. Customer will be billed as per scheduled billing plan of £125 last day of each month.

How can I apply a billing plan to delivery relevant sales line?

entry.title: Re: Stay certified without learning hub
entry.content:
Support confirmed that a learning hub subscription is necessary in order to stay certified. This seems very odd since a subscription is far more expensive than a single certification exam.

If I remeber correctly SAP said by than that a certification will not lose it's validity. This does not seem the case anymore...

Kr

 

@Gopinath

entry.title: Re: Reset clear items from previous FIscal year
entry.content:
This message could be converted to a warning one or even deactivated

  • Check 395526 - FBRA: no warning message when clearing previous years
  • SM30 on T100S or thru SPRO

entry.title: Re: Detailed steps to establish integration between Azure blob Storage & BTP CPI
entry.content: Do we need to configure cloud connector for connecting AzureStorage or its not required.

entry.title: Re: SSO Certificate Renewal process for SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi

The correct decision would be to migrate to IAS. There's a whole community about this migration. You can start here to understand the process

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/568fdf1f14f14fd089a3cd15194d19cc/53aad36ddbf242dd8e9e6d09804c11e0.html

the main community is here and they schedule calls parodically to answer questions about this migration to customers and partners. not sure if they still have calls at this point.

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/migration-to-sap-cloud-identity-authentication-ias-ips-innovation-alert-amp/ba-p/271407

The migration is not complex but you need to evaluate some things depending on the customer landscape. i.e how many instances do you have and how many IAS tenants you need. How will you manage PWD based access for non-SSO users if you have such scenario, etc. 

IAS in your scenario probably will be used just as a proxy if you want to maintain your IDP. This means that when a user logs to SF will be redirected to IAS first but IAS will then authenticate the user with your IDP. The SSO Users should not see any impact, there are still authenticating with your IDP but behind the scenes you use IAS as a proxy

Hope it Helps

Diego

entry.title: Re: Dynamic restricted key figure in analytic model
entry.content:
Thanks

I tried to add to mi time dimension calculated columns like LastMonth etc. abut when I tried to build a restricted measure over then time dimension with a filter like MONTH = LastMonth (both columns from the same time dimesion) it I got a message saying "Right side operand LastMonth must not be a dimension
And when I tried to build something like Month(BillDate()) = LastMonth I got a message "functions must be used in the restricted measure expression"

entry.title: Re: Reset clear items from previous FIscal year

entry.title: Re: Backorder Processing in aATP - SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi @nipunr ,

Thanks for the detailed explanation on strategies with an example.
We have a client requirement for BOP where in BOP run should follow a first-come, first served approach, where orders that fall into Back orders should be confirmed based on their creation sequence. Selection criteria is Material and plants combination. Additionally, any already confirmed orders (Partially or fully) should not lose their confirmations- they should either retain their existing confirmation or gain additional stock if available?
Given these conditions, which BOP strategy in aATP would best fulfill requirement?

entry.title: Re: How to delete the inactive transformation from the productive environment

We will open it in SE06 and delete it manually. I tried to do exaclty your described steps. After several retries I stopped it. Have you had similar problems?

Regards,

Peter

entry.title: How to handle variables in SQL based view - Data Sphere
entry.content:
Hi Guys,SAP HANA cloud

Need your expertise in handling the variables in SQL based view. I want to read the values of specific record's fields from the prior record into variables and then want to search a record based on those variables.

Tried using below script but not assigning any values into LINK_LEVEL, FATH_COMP, FATH_GPC, SON_COMP, SON_GPC.

 

DECLARE LV_REC_COUNT INTEGER;
DECLARE LV_INDEX INTEGER;
DECLARE LV_NEST_INDEX INTEGER;
DECLARE LV_INDEX_TEST INTEGER;

DECLARE LV_FATH_COMP NVARCHAR(8);
DECLARE LV_SON_COMP NVARCHAR(8);
DECLARE LV_FATH_GPC NVARCHAR(18);
DECLARE LV_SON_GPC NVARCHAR(18);

-- DECLARE LT_TEMP TABLE ( CONS_SET VARCHAR(2), SU_BUNIT VARCHAR(2) );

LT_COST_03_DATA = SELECT * FROM "APMR_ST_COST_03";
SELECT COUNT(*) INTO LV_REC_COUNT FROM :LT_COST_03_DATA;

LT_COST_INDEX = SELECT *, ' ' AS LINK_LEVEL, ' ' AS FATH_COMP, ' ' AS FATH_GPC, ' ' AS SON_COMP, ' ' AS SON_GPC, ROW_NUMBER() OVER ( PARTITION BY TOLL_FLAG ORDER BY FRS_CODE, CALMONTH, NFRD_GPNO ) AS ROWNUM FROM :LT_COST_03_DATA;

LT_COST_LINK_FINAL = SELECT * FROM :LT_COST_INDEX WHERE 1 = 100;

LV_INDEX = 1;
LV_NEST_INDEX = 1;

WHILE (LV_INDEX <= 2 )
DO
LV_INDEX = LV_INDEX + 1;
LT_INDEX_RECORD = SELECT * FROM :LT_COST_INDEX WHERE ROWNUM = LV_INDEX;
IF RECORD_COUNT(:LT_INDEX_RECORD) <> 0 THEN
WHILE (LV_NEST_INDEX <= 20) DO
IF LV_NEST_INDEX = 1 THEN
SELECT "FRS_CODE" INTO LV_FATH_COMP FROM :LT_INDEX_RECORD WHERE ROWNUM = LV_INDEX;
SELECT "NFRD_GPNO" INTO LV_FATH_GPC FROM :LT_INDEX_RECORD WHERE ROWNUM = LV_INDEX;
SELECT "FRS_FPUR" INTO LV_SON_COMP FROM :LT_INDEX_RECORD WHERE ROWNUM = LV_INDEX;
SELECT "NFRD_GPNO" INTO LV_FATH_GPC FROM :LT_INDEX_RECORD WHERE ROWNUM = LV_INDEX;
LT_COST_LINK_FINAL =
SELECT * FROM :LT_INDEX_RECORD
UNION
SELECT * FROM :LT_COST_LINK_FINAL;
ELSE
LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD = SELECT * FROM :LT_COST_INDEX WHERE
"FRS_CODE" = LV_SON_COMP AND
"NFRD_GPNO" = LV_SON_GPC;
IF RECORD_COUNT(:LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD) <> 0 THEN
SELECT "FRS_CODE" INTO LV_FATH_COMP FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD WHERE "FRS_CODE" = LV_SON_COMP AND
"NFRD_GPNO" = LV_SON_GPC;
SELECT "NFRD_GPNO" INTO LV_FATH_GPC FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;
SELECT "FRS_FPUR" INTO LV_SON_COMP FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;
SELECT "NFRD_GPNO" INTO LV_FATH_GPC FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;

SELECT REPLACE( "LINK_LEVEL", '' , LV_NEST_INDEX ) FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;
SELECT REPLACE( "FATH_COMP", '' , LV_FATH_COMP ) FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;
SELECT REPLACE( "FATH_GPC", '' , LV_FATH_GPC ) FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;
SELECT REPLACE( "SON_COMP", '' , LV_SON_COMP ) FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;
SELECT REPLACE( "FATH_GPC", '' , LV_FATH_GPC ) FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;

LT_COST_LINK_FINAL =
SELECT * FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD
UNION
SELECT * FROM :LT_COST_LINK_FINAL;
END IF;
END IF;
LV_NEST_INDEX = LV_NEST_INDEX + 1;
END WHILE;
LV_NEST_INDEX = 1;
ELSE
END IF;
END WHILE;

RETURN :LT_COST_LINK_FINAL;

-----------------------------

Can some one let me know how to get the values of specific fields of specific record into variables or so.

Appreciate your help, thanks.

Regards,

Santosh T.

entry.title: Re: Unleash Your Inner ABAP Rockstar with Joule for developers , ABAP AI capabilities
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there any chance to have this activated on the ABAP trial (TRL) instance?

Thanks,

Peter

entry.title: Re: Get log text messages from slg1 tcode
entry.content:
The meaning of message ID and message number (SY-MSGID, SY-MSGNO) can be looked up in transaction SE91. (Here "message ID" is called "message class". This can be a bit confusing, but it's just another word for the same thing...)

For example, if you specify Message Class = D0 and Message Number = 004, SE91 will tell you that the error text is:
"&: Act. table parameter & is empty"

You then replace the placeholders "&" with the values of SY-MSGV1 and SY-MSGV2. For example with MSGV1 = "Warning" and MSGV2 = "ORDER_HEADER_IN" we would get the final error message as
"Warning: Act. table parameter ORDER_HEADER_IN is empty"

Function module MESSAGE_PREPARE can be used to format a message text (=fill all the placeholders). As input it takes the language, message ID, No, V1-V4 and as output it returns the final error message.

entry.title: Re: BAPI or function module to delete technical objects f...
entry.content:
Did you already check BAPI_CUSTOMERCONTRACT_CHANGE options available for your version

  • Check also KBA 3323446 - Sales - SD BAPIs - Standard behaviors guide and How-to scenario 
  • But be aware of restriction on SD BAPI such as 1795864 - Serial number in sales order processing using BAPIs and usage of FM such as SERNR_AD_TO_AU 109533

entry.title: Re: Unleash Your Inner ABAP Rockstar with Joule for developers , ABAP AI capabilities
entry.content: @Peter_Inotai - no, J4D is available at no extra cost only for paying customers

entry.title: Re: How SAP B1 specifically in picking module create information from "bin location allocation
entry.content:
Hello,

Check the table OIBQ - Item Bin Accumulator Table. 

SELECT  OIBQ."BinAbs" AS "BinAbsEntry", OIBQ."OnHandQty" AS "Quantity"

FROM OIBQ

INNER JOIN OBIN ON OBIN."AbsEntry" = OIBQ."BinAbs"

WHERE OIBQ."ItemCode" = '99999'

AND OIBQ."WhsCode" = '01'

AND OIBQ."OnHandQty" > 0

Best regards

entry.title: Help with Adding Target Value to Sales Dashboard Query
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I’m working on creating a dashboard where I can filter sales data by month and display the total sales amount. So far, I’ve successfully achieved this. However, I’ve encountered an issue when trying to add a target value so that the dashboard not only shows the filtered sales but also displays a target value in a separate column next to it.

I haven’t been able to figure out how to implement this functionality. Below is the SQL query I have so far. Can anyone help me figure out how to modify it to include the target value in a new column?

SELECT
"CardCode" as "Customer Code",
"CardCode",
"CardName" AS "Customer Name",
"DocDate" as "Posting Date",
U."USER_CODE" AS "User Code",
"Total" as "Total Sales Amount",
"Total" as "Total Sales Amount"
FROM
(
(SELECT
T0."CardCode",
T0."CardName",
T0."DocDate",
T0."DocTotal" + IFNULL((SELECT SUM(t4."Gross") FROM inv9 t4 WHERE T0."DocEntry" = t4."DocEntry"), 0) AS "Total",
CASE
WHEN E1."userId" IS NULL THEN T0."UserSign"
ELSE E1."userId"
END AS "UserId"
FROM
oinv T0
LEFT JOIN
(
SELECT
"empID",
"userId",
"salesPrson"
FROM
OHEM
WHERE
"userId" IS NOT NULL AND "salesPrson" IS NOT NULL
) E1 ON T0."SlpCode" = E1."salesPrson"
WHERE
T0."CANCELED" = 'N'
)
UNION ALL
(SELECT
T1."CardCode",
T1."CardName",
T1."DocDate",
(-1) * (T1."DocTotal" + IFNULL((SELECT SUM(t5."Gross") FROM rin9 t5 WHERE T1."DocEntry" = t5."DocEntry"), 0)) AS "Total",
CASE
WHEN E1."userId" IS NULL THEN T1."UserSign"
ELSE E1."userId"
END AS "UserId"
FROM
orin T1
LEFT JOIN
(
SELECT
"empID",
"userId",
"salesPrson"
FROM
OHEM
WHERE
"userId" IS NOT NULL AND "salesPrson" IS NOT NULL
) E1 ON T1."SlpCode" = E1."salesPrson"
WHERE
T1."CANCELED" = 'N'
)
UNION ALL
(SELECT
T2."CardCode",
T2."CardName",
T2."DocDate",
"DocTotal" AS "Total",
CASE
WHEN E1."userId" IS NULL THEN T2."UserSign"
ELSE E1."userId"
END AS "UserId"
FROM
OCSI T2
LEFT JOIN
(
SELECT
"empID",
"userId",
"salesPrson"
FROM
OHEM
WHERE
"userId" IS NOT NULL AND "salesPrson" IS NOT NULL
) E1 ON T2."SlpCode" = E1."salesPrson"
WHERE
T2."CANCELED" = 'N'
)
UNION ALL
(SELECT
T3."CardCode",
T3."CardName",
T3."DocDate",
(-1) * "DocTotal" AS "Total",
CASE
WHEN E1."userId" IS NULL THEN T3."UserSign"
ELSE E1."userId"
END AS "UserId"
FROM
OCSV T3
LEFT JOIN
(
SELECT
"empID",
"userId",
"salesPrson"
FROM
OHEM
WHERE
"userId" IS NOT NULL AND "salesPrson" IS NOT NULL
) E1 ON T3."SlpCode" = E1."salesPrson"
WHERE
T3."CANCELED" = 'N'
)
) T
INNER JOIN
OUSR U ON T."UserId" = U."USERID"

entry.title: Re: Billing Number and XBLNR Number is not Generated Same...
entry.content:
Should be the same if you applied steps of KBA 3444642 - Determination of reference number (XBLNR) and assignment number (ZUONR) in billing document 

  • Check for any own data transfer routine that would override SAP values, or even some customer development that would not respect state of art
  • Look for available notes for your version
  • Raise an incident/case to SAP if you don't find

entry.title: Navigating the Future of Disclosure Management with SAP
entry.content:
Navigating the Future of Disclosure Management with SAP - Continued Support for SAP Disclosure Management in the Private Cloud & On Premises and Positioning a Partner-First Strategy for the Public Cloud 

In today's fast-paced business environment, the accurate and timely disclosure of financial and regulatory information is critical. Companies require robust and reliable solutions to manage this complex process seamlessly. SAP continues to offer solutions to support these needs, particularly in an on-premise and private cloud setup.  

SAP’s solution SAP Disclosure Management streamlines the final stages of financial reporting by enabling the creation, management, collaboration, and publication of financial data, while integrating compliance-enabling XBRL tagging and secure access to SAP data sources like SAP S/4HANA, SAP Business Warehouse, and SAP Sustainability Control Tower (more info here), as well as many other data sources such as SQL DB or spreadsheets. In this blog we provide an update on the current state of SAP Disclosure Management, reflecting the evolving strategies and offerings tailored to meet the diverse needs of businesses today. 

SAP Disclosure Management for On-Premise and Private Cloud Deployments 

SAP is committed to bring SAP Disclosure Management to the private cloud in the near-term as an exciting advancement in SAP’s cloud first strategy. 

For businesses that prefer on-premise solutions, SAP Disclosure Management continues to be a trusted choice. This comprehensive solution enables enterprises to streamline their financial disclosure processes, ensuring compliance and enhancing transparency. We are pleased to inform you that maintenance for SAP Disclosure Management support has been confirmed until at least the entirety of 2030. This commitment underscores our dedication to providing long-term, stable support for our customers utilizing on-premise solutions. 

The chosen support period aligns with SAP's broader policy for many of our products and extensions. Maintenance extensions are typically reviewed three years before the end date. Notably, SAP Disclosure Management has historically seen several extensions, reflecting our commitment to adapt to evolving customer needs and regulatory landscapes. Businesses can continue to rely on SAP Disclosure Management with confidence, knowing we have their long-term interests covered. 

Partner-First Strategy for Public Cloud Solutions 

As digital transformation accelerates, many businesses are adopting public-cloud-based solutions to harness greater flexibility, scalability, and innovation. Recognising this shift, SAP will embrace a partner-first strategy for the public cloud solutions in the disclosure management domain. This approach will foster a vibrant ecosystem where SAP partners can develop and deliver specialised disclosure management solutions tailored to the unique needs of various industries and regions. 

Customers looking for public cloud solutions can explore partner offerings available on the SAP Store. Here, you will find innovative and reliable partner solutions designed to integrate with your SAP environment, helping you to meet your disclosure management requirements effectively. 

Conclusion 

The landscape of financial disclosure is complex and ever-changing. However, with SAP and partner offerings, businesses can confidently manage their disclosure processes both now and in the future, whether you prefer an on-premise or private cloud environment -- or the agility of public cloud offerings through our partner ecosystem. 

For more information or to start your journey with SAP Disclosure Management, contact your account executive or visit the SAP Store. Let SAP and our trusted partners help you navigate the future of disclosure management with confidence. 

Disclaimer: Maintenance timelines and product availability are subject to change based on SAP's review and extension policies. SAP customers should always refer to the latest official SAP communications and the SAP Product Availability Matrix available at SAP for Me for the most current information.

entry.title: Re: SAP SIGNAVIO PROCESS INTELLIGENCE: Unkown IP adress a...
entry.content:
Hello @mrankov ,

Thanks for posting this question! My name is Ascha and I am part of the virtual Customer Success Team at SAP Signavio.

After aligning with our Support Team, we do recommend you to open a ticket via SAP for Me (Login via S-User ID and Password) as it seems like this is an individual issue. By creating a ticket, you'll be in direct contact with our experts who are eager to support you with your problem.

Thanks for your understand and kind regards

Ascha (as part of the virtual Customer Success Team)

you need to assign the control key for SO 0051 and for PO 0054

 

mohamed

Let´s wait.

entry.title: Re: Future Approaches for Replacing SAP Disclosure Manage...
entry.content: Also check out our recent blog post "Navigating the Future of Disclosure Management with SAP" at https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blogs-by-sap/navigating-the-future-of-disclosure-management-with-sap/ba-p/14021434

entry.title: Re: Print HU label at Product WT confirmation in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Markas ,

could you please clarify the following:

Usually, multiple warehouse tasks are confirmed into the same picking handling unit. If printing shall take place at confirmation of the warehouse task, would you then expect that it is printed for every confirmation? So it would be more like a material label for each pick? Or should it only print at the last warehouse task? This would be impossible for the system to know if a warehouse task is the last warehouse task. Or is your use case simply, that there is always just one warehouse task?

Thank you for further clarifying the context.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: Release of SAP Disclosure Management Stack 2200
entry.content:
To get a view on future plans of SAP in the area of financial disclosure, also check out our recent blog post
Navigating the Future of Disclosure Management with SAP

entry.title: Re: REFX integration with PM/ FSM
entry.content: Thank you for the details, Andre...

entry.title: Re: LINK TO WEBGUI IN TUTORIAL IS NOT WORKING ANYMORE?
entry.content:
A hyperlink is not an image so don't post a screenshot, post the URL.

Got it.

 

In this step you’ll find the EPM OData service by looking for it via the maintenance transaction for the Internet Communication Framework (ICF), to understand how web-based resources in general and OData services in particular are managed within an ABAP system.

Log on to the SAP Gateway Demo system via the Web GUI. If necessary, use the arrow button to make the OK Code field appear, so you can enter transaction codes.

You were already told one week ago that the Web GUI doesn't work, there's only an access via Fiori LaunchPad. Skip the section or contact SAP to make it fixed (feedback button).

entry.title: Re: Print HU label at Product WT confirmation in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hey @Christian,

We only print 1 label per pick (WT).

We pack only 1 material per HU (carton). We will never add more than 1 material to this HU.

An example of how our HUs will look like,

HU 300000501, Material: TG11, Qty x 3

entry.title: Re: Destination - Additional Properties
entry.content: Is it possible to add more properties which are not in the list provided?

entry.title: Re: Print HU label at Product WT confirmation in S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Automate credential management for SDI Agent Admin and Agent XS Messaging User
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

Can you please guide us around automation steps that can be used to update the Agent Admin HANA User and the Agent XS HANA User credentials?

Is there any SAP Standard API or command line utility or script available which can be used to update the credential store on the DPAgent host machine and/or update credentials for both these users?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Automate credential management for SDI Agent Admin an...
entry.content:
Dear Team,

There is no built-in tool to fully automate these processes, but both the Configuration Agent and the Secure Storage Utility can be used in batch mode. You can find help for these utilities here:

  • Manage the Agent XS HANA User Credentials [Batch]
  • Connecting to SAP HANA [Batch]

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Which course should i take to clear the fiori developer certification?
Re: Supply Protection of advanced ATP in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @arindamsur 
no this is not correct.
Protection is the quantity you can define for a single time bucket of a protection group. Usually a request doesn't match with multiple supply protection objects (or Groups).
The important thing here is not the protection, but the restriction. If a request (e.g. a Sales Order) doesn't match a characteristic of the Supply Protection Object the request is restricted by all the remaining protection of SUP. Also later weeks are restricting the request. Only if a time bucket is in the past (end date is in the past), the protection will get ignored and will not restrict.

If a request matches a time bucket (with its characteristics and the matching date) it depends on the settings of SUP, how other time buckets are restricting the request. This is also described (and illustrated) in this Blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/supply-protection-in-a-nutshell/ba-p/13580060

Best regards,

Arno

entry.title: Re: Destination - Additional Properties
entry.content: Hi s_satyamsetti, yes, you can add any values you like. You are not required to use the values from the combo box.

entry.title: Re: Data not updated in CDS view

entry.title: Re: 🚨 SAP Build Apps – Access Denied (403 Forbidden) – S...

entry.title: Re: Mongo ODBC Connection - failing to connect, Crystal 2...
entry.content:
I suggest contacting Atlas and asking them if they have any special configuration settings to connect to Crystal Reports.

Note: Cr 2020 is a 64 bit application.

Quick test.

Right click on crw64.exe and select Run as Administrator.

Don't open a report, click on File, Log on/off server, ODBC, your 64 bit DSN, fill in the log on info and see if that works. Sometimes existing reports or the report wizard can cause issues when connecting.

FYI - Mongo DB or Atlas is not listed in the Database PAM for CR, but generic ODBC clients typically do work. something in their DLL is causing CR to crash:

Faulting module name: atsql.dll, version: 1.4.2.0, time stamp: 0x675af79d

entry.title: Exchange Property management in Split-Gather blocks
entry.content:
Exchange Properties created before the Split-Gather block will be available within and after the block. However, if an Exchange Property is created within the Split-Gather block, there is a high chance its value will be replaced during the last execution of the Split. The probability of overwriting or data loss increases when Parallel Processing is used.

To capture information within the Split-Gather block without overwriting or missing values, the solution is to create Exchange Properties with a random naming pattern.

Here is a sample iFlow that demonstrates how to create dynamic Exchange Properties using UUID and then collect values in these properties.

Here are the configurations of Splitter - Gather and Content Modifier.

Groovy script for CreateProperties

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.util.UUID
import java.time.Instant

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def now = Instant.now().toString()
    def CamelSplitIndex = message.getProperty('CamelSplitIndex')
    message.setProperty("p_ExchangeProperty_${UUID.randomUUID()}", "now:${now} \t CamelSplitIndex:${CamelSplitIndex}\r\n")
    return message
}

Groovy script for CollectProperties

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def concatenatedString = message.getProperties().findAll { it.key.startsWith('p_ExchangeProperty') }.values().join('')
    message.setProperty('p_ConcatenatedString', concatenatedString)
    return message
}

Result: Exchange Properties are created with in the Split-Gather block and we are able to store data into properties without data loss.

entry.title: Re: Does Basic Syntax support the Format function?
entry.content:
Once in CR Designer hit the F1 key to open the help file and search for Round:

Basic and Crystal syntax.

Overloads

  • Round (x)

  • Round (x, #places)

entry.title: Program FAGL_CL_REGROUP
entry.content:
Program FAGL_CL_REGROUP regroups vendors with a debit balance and customers with a credit balance.

However, when running it, open customer payments are also taken into account for regrouping, even if the customer balance is positive. This doesn't apply to all customer payments, but only to some.

There is no apparent logic to why some open payments for customers with positive balance are considered, while others are not.

Have you experienced this before?

Thank you

entry.title: Multi currency for a customer
entry.content:
Hi ,

Please let me know if we can maintain Multiple Currency for a Single Customer.

Same customer biils in different currency

 

Rg

Siva

entry.title: Re: BTP HANA temporarily unavailable
entry.content: Hey If you have this kind of problem try using a different browser or try going incognitio mode on chrome and this might work. I just copied the url and opened it on safari and it worked. I also tried to go incognitio on chrome and it also worked. Also another solution might be to clear your local cache on chrome

entry.title: Re: Static Factory Actions with Deep Parameters don't work
entry.content: which line of code failed?

entry.title: How to interrupt Fiori application loading at startup (Fiori Elements, OData V4)?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have to implement the following business requirement: There's a SPRO setting that enables or disables my custom Fiori application, and I must interrupt (or proceed with) application loading based on this parameter. The app is built using Fiori Elements, OData V4 and I'm utilizing Flexible Programming Model wherever possible.

I have tried a few approaches, but none seems to work. Two of them are as follows:

  • I override onInit (or onBeforeRendering) methods in the List Page controller extension, where I read SPRO setting from service (it works), display an error message box (also works), but the app keeps loading in the background, which is what I want to avoid. In freestyle apps I used the trick with a placeholder with autoClose setting set to false and closing it if the app is enabled in SPRO, but in case of Fiori Elements app this placeholder seems to have no effect on the app in the manifest...
  • I created a custom "landing page" with the message that the app is disabled, but if it's enabled, I planned to navigate to the List Page immediately. Again, querying backend works, the page is loading and displaying my message, but I simply can't force navigation to the List Page if the app is enabled...

I would sincerely appreciate if you share your ideas about how to deal with that requirement.

Regards,

Mykyta

entry.title: Re: Controlling Area in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: There was a conscious decision to deliver only one controlling area in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. This had to do with harmonizing the Finance applications and supporting a group perspective for management reporting while allowing intercompany cost allocations, which can only take place within a single controlling area. In parallel we looked at the historic reasons why customers had used different controlling areas in the past (e.g. using the controlling area currency as a functional currency rather than a group currency, splitting the controlling area to handle different fiscal year variants,...) and rearchitected the solution to extend the number of supported currencies and assign different fiscal year variants in the non-leading ledger.

entry.title: Re: where to create new Relative DATE FUNCTION
entry.content: You mean, like "FIRSTDAYOFCURRENTMONTH", ""FIRSTDAYOFCURRENTQUARTER", etc. (comment for indexing your question)

entry.title: Re: valuated stock in transit for scope item BDN
entry.content:
Hi, 

We had the same problem and yes there is still no standard solution to the scenario. So we build a work around to convert the BDN process to BJE (Make-to-order) + BD9:

Work around steps as below:

1. Set Strategy group 20 and procurement key F in the material master

2. Scheduling MRP run

3. Trigger Outbund delivery creation from Sales order based on goods receipt of the Purchase order (triggered from BJE)

5MQ setup now works as the item category now is TAN instead of CBAB. 

Best regards

Dave

entry.title: Understanding Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In the fast-paced world of cloud ERP, ensuring the continuous health and efficiency of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system is crucial. That’s where Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) come into play. Our new e-learning, “Unlocking Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition,” provides you with an overview of the available CQCs for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and explains how and when to request these services.

Why Are CQCs Important?

Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) services are part of the SAP Enterprise Support, cloud edition support agreement which is embedded in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition subscription and does not require additional fees. These services help organizations proactively assess their systems, identify potential risks, and make sure you are following best practices from SAP.

CQCs are remote services that help reduce implementation risks and analyze your system and/or situation with an aim of providing improvement recommendations. With each CQC service you will be provided with an individualized detailed services report, which includes:

  • Management Summary  -  containing key findings at a glance
  • Action Plan  -  high level overview of the required actions identified from the service
  • Detailed Recommendations - containing all the findings, recommendations and required actionable items

CQCs for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition play a vital role in keeping your project on track throughout the SAP Activate phases, offering:

  • Tailored recommendations specific to your project
  • Best practices to ensure you are following best practices from SAP
  • Enablement resources for continuous improvement

What You’ll Discover in the E-Learning

Our e-learning module provides:

  • A clear introduction to CQC services
  • An overview of the specific CQC services available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Guidance on when and how to request CQCs to maximize their benefits

How to Access the E-Learning

Ready to take advantage of CQCs for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition? Listen to our e-learning session to get started.

📌Access the E-Learning Here

Sign up for the Value Map for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to access the full list of CQCs and receive personalized guidance on your cloud ERP journey.

🚀How to Access Value Maps in 3 Easy Steps:

  1. Sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support (please allow up to 2 hours for processing).
  2. Access Value Maps via the Learning Hub.
  3. Navigate to the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Here, you can collaborate in forums, ask questions, or use the “Request a Call” feature to speak with an expert.

Value maps are intuitive to navigate, but we recommend scheduling an onboarding call to tailor the experience to your needs and highlight the most relevant content.

Have Questions?

  • Use the “Ask Questions” feature within the Value Maps.
  • Contact us at sapesvaluemaps@sap.com.

Brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support Value Map team for SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

entry.title: Re: Manage your project documentation using SAP Cloud ALM

it is also planned for other elements. but we might start with documents as a first element being cross-project available. you can vote for this in the influence request: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/319126

Cheers

Sinje

entry.title: Re: Dynamic restricted key figure in analytic model
entry.content:
Hi @shlomi_weiss 

I think you did not understand what I meant... you don't have to use a time dimension, but it is a new formula column in e.g. your fact view. 

Then you will use this characteristic in your restricted key figure in the AM.

entry.title: Is it possible to change the name of performa invoice ?
entry.content:
Hi All,

we have a scenario in which we have to create a delivery in advance to print out the documents for custom clearance. My question is that we are going to create a perform invoice  against a delivery since at the time of documenatation we will not do a goods issue. Is it possible and Ok from process point of view to rename the performa invoice to an actual invoice so to get a custom clearnace? as for custom clearance cannot be done on a document with a header "Performa".

entry.title: Re: How Can I buy SAP SQL Anywhere 17 License
entry.content: Be aware, LTA, that Novalys can only distribute the "authenticated" version of SQL Anywhere. This means data updates can only be issued from either an appointed application, or from ISQL. Other connections can retrieve data. Can anyone listening here from SAP give us any idea if and when SQL Anywhere licenses will be available from the SAP Store? An indeterminate answere is hard to work with when you have a business to run and customers to give commitments to.

Excellent, I want to try it but from what I read it is only available for customers and partners through a license, it is not available for freelance developers by other means.

Will it be available for BTP Trial or Free tier?

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP Node.js - Insert vector embedding data into h...
entry.content:
Hi Dinu,

wow, it's really an amazing change.

It does work after making change as you suggested, Thank you so much.

BTW, due to this issue previously, I found another workaround by using the trigger of Hana Cloud. So I will try what you said later.

Thank you again.

entry.title: Re: Unable to write workflow container in WF Exit
entry.content:
The problem was that I used a different table definition when writing the container. The container defined in the workflow had a different structure.

Writing the the wrong table into the container worked perfectly; no errors. But when transferring the workitem container back to the workflow container, the type mismatch was detected. I did not see any error somewhere. I recognized by chance that I was using the wrong table structure. After using the correct structure, it worked properly.

entry.title: Re: Manual Selection of Production version Not working
entry.content: In case you are on S/4, you also need to set 'BOM Explosion' to '2' (see 3051827 - In S/4 HANA there is no popup when converting a planned order into a production order. - SAP for Me)

entry.title: Question: How to restrict access to EnableNow in regards to licensing (SuccessFactors & EnableNow)
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

We have the issue that we want to roll out EnableNow for a limited population only. Yet, in regards to licenses, we do not see any clean solution to restrict users in SuccessFactors not to access EnableNow (via SSO), so users outside the population do not generate costs just by accidentally opening the SAP Companion via the question mark button at the top. 
Is there any technical solution to this issue?

entry.title: Re: Print HU label at Product WT confirmation in S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Hi @Markas ,

since you want to print during warehouse task confirmation, have you already tried to work with warehouse order documents? I would assume, this form also has all the information you need and you can adjust the form layout as needed. The printing should be called also during warehouse task confirmation.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Partial goods issue in S4HANA (non-cloud)
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a delivery where I have to pick 20 Kgs, for some reason 10 Kgs are missing due to late production.

Can I do a partial goods issue for 10 kgs ? or I need to adjust the qty of delivery to 10 and do a goods issue for a complete delivery and create a new one for the pending quantity? 

Please suggest, can we do a partial goods issue in SAP standard S4HANA?

Nico

Do you think this possible?

Regards,
Reza

entry.title: Configuring Serial Number Usage for Movement Types
entry.content:
Introduction

In inventory management, serial numbers play a crucial role in tracking and managing stock. Depending on your business requirements, you might need more flexibility in handling serial numbers to track and manage stock effectively. You can now define a different serial number usage for different movement types.

This blog post will guide you through how you can do this in your configuration environment using the configuration activity Define Serial Number Usage for Movement Types (106819). 

Why Configure Serial Number Usage? 

By default, serial number settings are defined in the serial number profile within the material master data. However, business scenarios such as goods receipts and goods issues may require different serial number handling. For instance, you might want optional serial numbers for goods receipts but mandatory ones for goods issues. This configuration allows for such flexibility. 

Prerequisites: 

  • Roles and Authorizations:  
    • For the Manage Your Solution app: The Business Process Expert is required. 
    • For the Customer Data Browser app: The Administrator (ADMINISTRATOR_DANA) user role is required.  It is included in the business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_CDB_PC. 
    • The business catalog SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_LO_SER_PC (Serial Number Management - Configuration) must be assigned to your business role.   

 

Configuration Steps 

This configuration scenario is a two-step process: 

1. First, you must create a movement type group for your serial number profile which contains your desired serial number usage. For more information, see below or visit the SAP Help Portal: Step 1: Define Serial Number Usage for Movement Type 

2. Second, you assign the movement type group to the movement type. For more information, see below or visit the SAP Help Portal:  Step 2: Assign Group to Movement Type. 

 

Before You Start 

Before getting started, it is helpful to collect the required information from an existing material document that represents the process where you would like to change the serial number usage. We recommend the following: 

  1. Find a recent material document that represents the relevant process and copy the material document number. 
  2. With the user ADMINISTRATOR_DANA, enter the Customer Data Browser app.
  3. Choose the CDS Views table at the top. 
  4. Search for I_MATERIALDOCUMENTITEM_2 and select. 
  5. Use the search filters to find your material document. 
  6. Make a screenshot or copy the relevant fields for reference in the configuration activity.

Relevant fields for reference might include: Material Document, Number of Material Document, Material Number, Plant, Consumption Posting, GOODSMOVEMENTTYPEREFDOCTYPE, Special Stock Indicator, Special Stock Type, Receipt Indicator, and Movement Type. 

Step 1: Define Serial Number Usage for Movement Type 

In your configuration environment, use the search function to open the following activity: Define Serial Number Usage for Movement Types (106819). For more information, see Configuration Environment of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

  1. Select the New Entries button to create a movement type group. 
  2. In the Movement Type Group column, create a 4-character movement type group name.
  3. Enter the relevant serial number profile. 
  4. In the Serial Number Usage column, use the drop down to select a usage type. 

     

Step 2: Assign Group to Movement Type

In the Assign Group to Movement Type segment, you can assign your newly created movement type group. 

  1. Select the Assign Group to Movement Type segment. 
  2. Find the movement type for the process where you require a different serial number usage. 
  3. In the Serial Number Movement Type Group column (Movement Type Group) of the relevant movement type, add your newly created movement type group. 

  4.  

    If required, change the Receipt Indicator and the Consumption Posting to fit your needs. 

For more details, please see Define Serial Number Usage for Movement Types on the SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: Re: Print HU label at Product WT confirmation in S/4HANA...
entry.content: We thought about this, however, we need to print the HUs at the task level not the Warehouse Order level. I believe? I've seen a Badi in Private that allows us to extend OPD from the Warehouse Order level to the WT task level instead but I don't think this is available in public. We have 50 picks (WT) per WO.

entry.title: Document Information Extraction – Understanding Schemas
entry.content:
What is a Document Information Extraction Schema? 

A schema contains a list of header fields and line items representing the target information that you want to extract from a particular type of document. You must select a schema when you add documents to the Document Information Extraction UI. When uploading documents to the Document Information Extraction service via the Document API, it’s not mandatory to add a schema to the Options Payload, but we strongly recommend this approach since it allows you to extract custom fields from documents along with the standard fields, and also benefit from generative AI. You can find lists of the standard fields in Extracted Header Fields and Extracted Line Items. 

 

Custom Schemas and SAP Schemas 

You can either create your own schema from scratch (for either standard or custom document types) or use a preconfigured SAP schema. The Document Information Extraction service provides SAP schemas for the following standard document types: 

  • Invoice ("schemaName": "SAP_invoice_schema"; "schemaId": "cf8cc8a9-1eee-42d9-9a3e-507a61baac23") 
  • Payment advice ("schemaName": "SAP_paymentAdvice_schema"; "schemaId": "b7fdcfac-7853-42bb-89d2-ede2ba1ce803") 
  • Purchase order ("schemaName": "SAP_purchaseOrder_schema"; "schemaId": "fbab052e-6f9b-4a5f-b42f-29a8162eb1bf") 

In addition, there’s an SAP schema for custom documents (SAP_OCROnly_schema). You can use these SAP schemas unchanged to upload documents. If you don’t want to configure your own schema, simply select the appropriate SAP schema when you upload a document to the Document Information Extraction service. No configuration is needed when you use SAP schemas in this way. In fact, it’s not possible to change or delete original SAP schemas. Alternatively, you can use the Document Information Extraction UI to copy a suitable SAP schema and edit the default fields in line with your needs.  

If you prefer to consume the Document Information Extraction service via API only, you can do the following using the respective endpoints: 

  • Use the relevant schemaId value to see details of the associated SAP schema, including the list of standard fields it contains (Schema API endpoint GET /schemas/{schemaId}). 
  • Create a new custom schema (Schema API endpoint POST /schemas endpoint)  
  • Use the SAP schema details to add standard fields to your new custom schema in line with your needs. Alternatively, add custom fields (Schema API endpoint POST /schemas/{schemaId}/versions/{version}/fields). 

 

Schema Versions 

Finally, when you configure, save, and activate a new schema, Document Information Extraction saves it automatically as version 1. You can use this initial version as the basis for creating additional versions of the same schema. All schema versions share the same name. 

Using versions is helpful if you process documents that have many of the fields provided in the original schema but also include others. You can also use different labels, descriptions, data types, and setup types for fields in different versions. 

 

See also: 

  • Schema API 
  • Schema Configuration
  • Schema Configuration: Best Practices 
  • Extraction Using Generative AI: Best Practices
  • Tutorial Mission: Shape Machine Learning to Process Custom Business Documents
  • Tutorial Mission: Shape Machine Learning to Process Standard Business Documents
  • Tutorial Mission: Use Generative AI to Process Business Documents

entry.title: Re: error when trying to connect to public cloud connecti...
entry.content:
Hi @tridwip ,

Did you follow the steps outlined here: https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#abap?

The reason I asked this is some steps might be missing in your installation so that ADT cannot connect to your system.

Regards,

George

entry.title: overview of delivery notes and PO in one layout
entry.content:
Hello 

Hpw can i see overview of delivery notes and POs in one transaction?

Of course with material numbers and quantities

Thank you

 

Ivan

entry.title: 🇩🇪 Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model and SAP generative AI Hub
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and SAP generative AI Hub. It is beneficial if you have some basic knowledge about JavaScript as we're using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant.

This CodeJam introduces attendees to aspects of exposing generative artificial intelligence (genAI) capabilities to execute tasks using a given business context.

During this event, you'll become acquainted with the theory behind genAI, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service.

While working through the exercises, you'll become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them into the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday May 15th, 2025 in Herne, Germany 🇩🇪 from 10:00 am - 4:00 pm. The workshop will be hosted by rku.it GmbH. The event will be held in english.

The instructor will be the Developer Advocate @KRiedelsheimer 

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome.

Please note that seating is limited - we can accomodate 25 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.
